# UC-17.3.6 · Policy Drift Detection

## Description

Zero-trust policies require continuous validation. Drift from baseline configuration introduces security gaps.

## Value

Zero-trust policies require continuous validation. Drift from baseline configuration introduces security gaps.

## Implementation

Track all ZT policy changes via audit logs. Compare current configuration against approved baseline. Alert on unauthorized modifications. Require change management approval for policy changes. Report on policy change frequency.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_zscaler`, Netskope Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 3808), `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (Prisma Access), `TA-fortinet_fortigate` (FortiSASE), Check Point App for Splunk (Splunkbase 4293).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: ZT policy audit logs, configuration snapshots.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Track all ZT policy changes via audit logs. Compare current configuration against approved baseline. Alert on unauthorized modifications. Require change management approval for policy changes. Report on policy change frequency.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=zt sourcetype="zt:admin_audit"
| search action IN ("policy_modified","rule_added","rule_deleted","rule_disabled")
| table _time, admin, action, policy_name, details
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Policy Drift Detection** — Zero-trust policies require continuous validation. Drift from baseline configuration introduces security gaps.

Documented **Data sources**: ZT policy audit logs, configuration snapshots. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_zscaler`, Netskope Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 3808), `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (Prisma Access), `TA-fortinet_fortigate` (FortiSASE), Check Point App for Splunk (Splunkbase 4293). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: zt; **sourcetype**: zt:admin_audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=zt, sourcetype="zt:admin_audit". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Pipeline stage (see **Policy Drift Detection**): table _time, admin, action, policy_name, details
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Netskope CASB and tenant admin for the same users, apps, and time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (policy changes), Timeline (modification events), Bar chart (changes by admin), Single value (changes this week).

## SPL

```spl
index=zt sourcetype="zt:admin_audit"
| search action IN ("policy_modified","rule_added","rule_deleted","rule_disabled")
| table _time, admin, action, policy_name, details
| sort -_time
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (policy changes), Timeline (modification events), Bar chart (changes by admin), Single value (changes this week).
